title: secale segetale var. dighoricum
intro: Secale segetale var. dighoricum, also known as Dighoricum wild rye, is a subspecies of the wild rye plant that is native to certain regions of Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Secale segetale var. dighoricum include its ability to help reduce fever, alleviate congestion, and soothe sore throats. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions or decoctions of the dried roots and leaves, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
